text: Training-induced increases in VO2max are largely facilitated by expansion of red blood cell volume and an associated improvement in stroke volume.
Stroke volume adapts to endurance training independent of changes in red blood cell volume, representing a separate mechanism for VO2max improvement.
Individual variations in VO2max improvements with exercise training are likely related to variations in red blood cell volume expansion and stroke volume adaptation.

All previously untrained individuals will improve VO2max in response to endurance exercise training provided the stimulus exceeds a certain volume and/or intensity threshold — true non-responders do not exist if training dose is sufficient.
Very high VO2max values observed in elite athletes are associated with exceptionally large red blood cell volumes and stroke volumes.
